original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Varicose veins: diagnosis and management — Recommendations
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
original_registry_entry.population: Adults with varicose veins, lower-limb symptoms, skin changes, venous ulceration, or venous insufficiency context.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Primary care and vascular-service recognition and referral assessment.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for lower-limb pain, aching, discomfort, swelling, heaviness, itching, pigmentation, eczema, and chronic-venous-insufficiency context. It does not diagnose stasis dermatitis or venous eczema and does not generate referral or treatment. This batch additionally reviews adult symptom, complication, referral, duplex and pregnancy recommendations while preserving the previously registered chronic-venous-skin and ulcer-history sections. No diagnosis, imaging or treatment is generated. exact_sections.heading: Recommendation 1.2.2 — symptomatic veins and lower-limb skin changes
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting lower-limb pain, aching, discomfort, swelling, heaviness, itching, pigmentation, and eczema considered by the clinician in chronic venous insufficiency context.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendation 1.2.2 — venous leg-ulcer history and referral context
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting a current or healed venous leg ulcer and an already made vascular referral without generating diagnosis, referral, urgency, or treatment.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.2 — symptoms, progression and complications
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician documentation of pain, aching, discomfort, swelling, heaviness, itching, skin change, bleeding, thrombophlebitis, ulcer and patient questions without asserting complication.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.2 — vascular-service referral features
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording bleeding, troublesome symptoms, skin change, hard painful veins and active or healed ulcer context; referral remains a clinician decision adapted to local pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1 and 1.4.1–1.4.3 — duplex assessment and pregnancy
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-reviewed duplex results and pregnancy context without generating imaging, intervention or compression advice.

title: Varicose veins: diagnosis and management — Recommendations
publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
population: Adults with varicose veins, lower-limb symptoms, skin changes, venous ulceration, or venous insufficiency context.
intended_setting: Primary care and vascular-service recognition and referral assessment.
